source_pr.title: *: cleanup default sysvar value usage
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/36485

Problem Summary:

Previously a default install of TiDB showed several differences of current_value vs. default value when reading from `information_schema.variables_info`. A lot of these were subtle bugs.

### What is changed and how it works?

The default vs current value is stabilized, and a test is added to make sure that any new exceptions (should be very few) need to be explicitly handled.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [x] Contains variable changes
Minor changes to default values. Because most of the broken values have getter functions, it won't be user perceivable. 
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The default vs current value is stabilized, and a test is added to make sure that any new exceptions (should be very few) need to be explicitly handled. | stmt := tk.MustQuery(`SELECT variable_name, default_value, current_value FROM information_schema.variables_info WHERE current_value != default_value and default_value != '' ORDER BY variable_name`) (infoschema/tables_test.go)
failure_signature: The default vs current value is stabilized, and a test is added to make sure that any new exceptions (should be very few) need to be explicitly handled. | stmt := tk.MustQuery(`SELECT variable_name, default_value, current_value FROM information_schema.variables_info WHERE current_value != default_value and default_value != '' ORDER BY variable_name`) (infoschema/tables_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: The default vs current value is stabilized, and a test is added to make sure that any new exceptions (should be very few) need to be explicitly handled. | stmt := tk.MustQuery(`SELECT variable_name, default_value, current_value FROM information_schema.variables_info WHERE current_value != default_value and default_value != '' ORDER BY variable_name`) (infoschema/tables_test.go)
files: infoschema/tables_test.go, session/bootstrap.go, sessionctx/variable/noop.go, sessionctx/variable/session.go, sessionctx/variable/sysvar.go, util/sqlexec/utils.go, util/sqlexec/utils_test.go
